summary: Improved matching in the lbmthp movie/THP player code by typing static data, refining THP decode structs and signatures, naming file/buffer fields, and reshaping several helpers to better match codegen. The PR produced 3 new matches and 5 unmatched-item improvements in main/melee/lb/lbmthp, including full matches for lbMthp_8001F67C, lbMthp_8001F624, and .data, plus significant improvements to fn_8001E910 and fn_8001F2A4.

key_files.role: Main matching work: rewrote lbmthp helpers around typed Movieplayer access, named file/buffer fields, static assert macros, rate-table helper, and pointer-correct THPVideoDecode calls.

Header/type improvements: added dolphin/thp include, corrected THPDec_8032FD40 and THPVideoDecode prototypes, replaced raw fields with THPDec_8032FD40_Data, frame_buffers, curr_file_offset, file_entrynum, and rate_table.

Static data typing: renamed the static Movieplayer storage to lbl_804333E0 with a Movieplayer macro, added lbMthp_DataBlock, named assert/report strings, and made lbl_803BAFE8 a typed data-block member.
Call-site fix for THPVideoDecode: changed the fourth argument cast from s32 to void*.

what_changed: Changed THPVideoDecode declaration from taking an s32 fourth argument to taking a void* fourth argument, and updated lb_01F8.c and lbmthp.c call sites accordingly.
Changed THPDec_8032FD40 to take THPDec_8032FD40_Data* and replaced separate unk_9C/unk_A0/unk_A2/unk_A4 fields in THPDecComp with a typed THPDec_8032FD40_Data unk_9C member.
Renamed important THPDecComp and Movieplayer fields: unk_4C became frame_buffers, unk_120 became curr_file_offset, unk_128 became file_entrynum, and Movieplayer unk_12C became rate_table.
Added LBMTHP_ASSERT and LBMTHP_ASSERTREPORT macros that call __assert with the lbmthp.c filename string and, for report assertions, emit OSReport first.

Replaced embedded string literals and generic HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTREPORT use with named static strings such as lbl_803BADBC, lbl_803BADC8, lbl_803BADEC, str_Warning_frame_offsets_not_supported, and lbl_803BAFB8.
Typed the lbmthp static data region with lbMthp_DataBlock, including assert/report string storage and the sobj_desc at offset 0x238, with a STATIC_ASSERT size of 0x250.
Changed the static Movieplayer declaration to static struct lbl_804333E0_t lbl_804333E0 and defined Movieplayer as a macro alias to preserve source readability while matching the label.
Reworked fn_8001E910 to use a local streamPlayer pointer to Movieplayer, named file/buffer fields, explicit curr_file_offset updates, and a goto label named request_done.
Reworked fn_8001ECF4 to use local width, height, count, and var_r29 temporaries, assign frame_buffers, maintain curr_file_offset, and replace debug OSReport string literals with named static data.
Extracted the repeated rate-table-to-frame conversion logic from fn_8001F2A4 into a static inline helper named lbMthp_GetFrame.
Updated lbMthp_8001F410 signature parameter names to filename, rate_table, buf, heap_size, and loop, and used a local streamPlayer pointer plus a power pointer to improve codegen.
Bot report showed matched code improved by 476 bytes and matched data by 592 bytes for GALE01, with matched code rising to 67.16% and matched data to 39.41%.
smart_moves: Used a macro alias for Movieplayer over the real lbl_804333E0 static symbol, preserving readable code without losing exact static-label layout.
Typed the string/data blob as lbMthp_DataBlock and then exposed lbl_803BAFE8 through a casted member macro, a useful tactic when contiguous data contains both strings and structured data.
Introduced local streamPlayer pointers to reduce repeated global accesses and influence register allocation/codegen in functions like fn_8001E910 and lbMthp_8001F410.
Converted repeated frame-rate table logic into a static inline helper, which improved fn_8001F2A4 matching while keeping the original repeated behavior visible at call sites through inlining.
Corrected pointer-vs-integer function signatures instead of locally forcing casts, which fixed both the declaration and call sites for THPVideoDecode.
Named semantically clear fields only where evidence supported them: frame_buffers, curr_file_offset, file_entrynum, currPackedSize, and rate_table.
decomp_lessons: For matching, do not assume an unknown integral field should remain s32 when all call sites treat it as an address; update the prototype and the call sites together, as with THPVideoDecode's fourth parameter.
Static assert/report strings can affect data matching; replacing literal strings with named static symbols and custom assert macros may be required to match original data layout and call sequence.
When an unknown struct subregion is passed to a typed external function, replacing scattered primitive fields with the external typed struct can improve both documentation and matching.
For global singleton state, a local pointer variable can be important for register allocation and instruction ordering.
Inline helpers can improve readability without necessarily changing generated code when they represent repeated compiler-emitted logic.
Use STATIC_ASSERT aggressively after struct and data-block typing; this PR preserved struct lbl_804333E0_t at 0x1D8 and lbMthp_DataBlock at 0x250.
Contiguous .data that mixes strings and structs can be modeled as a single data-block type, with macros used to recover individual legacy labels.
naming_conventions: Movieplayer remains the readable alias for the actual static label lbl_804333E0.
Fields with recovered semantics were renamed using lower_snake_case or existing mixed style: frame_buffers, curr_file_offset, file_entrynum, rate_table, currPackedSize.
The helper extracted from frame-rate conversion was named with module prefix and action: lbMthp_GetFrame.
Named static strings mostly retain address-style lbl_803Bxxxx names when no semantic name was introduced, while one warning string became str_Warning_frame_offsets_not_supported.
Module-local assert macros were prefixed LBMTHP_ASSERT and LBMTHP_ASSERTREPORT.
The typed static data aggregate was named lbMthp_DataBlock.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed fn_8001E910 cancelflag parameter from bool to int, matching the updated function declaration and likely the ABI/codegen expected by HSD_DevComRequest callback usage.
Used explicit casts to void* for THPVideoDecode input buffers, including (void*) lbl_804335B8.unk94 and (void*) (data->frame_buffers[data->unk_90] + 4).
Inserted a request_done goto label in fn_8001E910 to reproduce control flow around the HSD_DevComRequest path.
Assigned through compound expressions such as streamPlayer->unk_138 = (var_r4 = 0) and streamPlayer->unk_110 = (var_r4 = 1) to influence ordering/register use.
Pulled width, height, count, and aligned values into locals in fn_8001EBF0/fn_8001ECF4 to match load order and arithmetic sequencing.
Represented the decode init header at offset 0x9C with THPDec_8032FD40_Data so stores became field writes like unk_9C.val1, unk_9C._pad, unk_9C.val2, and unk_9C.val0.
Replaced hard-coded string literals with static symbols to improve .data and code references.
Preserved alignment operations using ALIGN_32 and explicit bitmask alignment for buffer sizes.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed +476 matched-code bytes and +592 matched-data bytes. New full matches: lbMthp_8001F67C, lbMthp_8001F624, and main/melee/lb/lbmthp .data. Improvements: fn_8001E910 to 99.15%, fn_8001F2A4 to 98.35%, fn_8001ECF4 to 96.88%, .sdata to 14.29%, and lbMthp_8001F410 to 95.67%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further type THPDecComp fields still named unk_40, unk_68, unk_70, unk_74, unk_78, unk_80, unk_8C, unk_90, unk_94, unk_98, unk_100, unk_104, unk_108, unk_110, unk_11C, unk_130, unk_134, unk_138, unk_140, unk_144, or unk_148.
Search for additional call sites of THPVideoDecode to confirm all prototypes and casts use void* for the fourth parameter.
Look for PRs that replace lbl_803Bxxxx string labels in lbmthp with more semantic names after this data-block typing.
Investigate whether rate_table's type should become a more specific struct or pair array instead of u32* or void*.
Search for future matching improvements to fn_8001E910, fn_8001ECF4, fn_8001F2A4, and lbMthp_8001F410 after this PR.
